problem_statement: LLM-based agents applied to software engineering are unreliable and biased, with single-agent hallucinations considered essentially inevitable; the project addresses how to make AI-generated SE artefacts reliable through supervised, orchestrated collaboration among many agents with human intervention.
magnitude_evidence: "more than 40 independent LLMs are available at present"
insufficiency_evidence: "phenomena like hallucinations of a single agent are substantially inevitable, since they are linked to the fundamental inference mechanism in generative models"
solution_mechanisms: governance policy language (consensus, majority voting, human-driven)
expert user intervention in AI decisions
notes: Project-exhibition paper describing an in-progress EU project (first 3 months); mechanisms are proposed/planned rather than empirically evaluated.
